content:
===BEGIN===
Blockchain is a technology that records transactions securely without relying on a central bank or authority. Instead of one institution verifying and storing all transaction details, blockchain uses a network of computers—often called “nodes”—spread across the world. When a transaction occurs, it is grouped with others into a “block.” This block is then shared with the network, where the computers work together to confirm its validity through a process called consensus.

Once verified, the block is added to a chain of previous blocks, creating a permanent and unchangeable record. Because many independent nodes must agree on the transaction’s accuracy, it becomes nearly impossible to alter or fake records without detection. This decentralized approach reduces the risk of fraud and increases transparency, all without needing a central bank to oversee or control the process. For businesses, blockchain offers a trustworthy way to track assets and transactions in real time, securely and efficiently.  
===END===
